Title: TECHQA | Q2 passage 4

tem; bullettin; patches; googlebigfix TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Why are IBM BigFix security patches not yet available even though Microsoft has already announced them? 

ANSWER
Each month Microsoft releases security bulletins, including operating system and product application patches. Various other hardware and software vendors release patches periodically. However, patches can be released any time. The Microsoft monthly patch release is known as Patch Tuesday because it typically occurs every second Tuesday of the month. 

Within 24 hours of Microsoft's release, the BigFix team typically creates and releases English-language Fixlets to match specific Microsoft patch bulletins. Each Fixlet targets the specific components that are listed by the Microsoft patch. While patches are released by Microsoft, they are typically not available in the BigFix console until about 24 hours after Microsoft has released them. 

Therefore you may not be seeing them in your BigFix console because 24 hours have not passed yet. 

Customers can check Microsoft's site to see if the patch has been released at:
http://www.catalog.update.microsoft.com/Home.aspx [http://www.catalog.update.microsoft.com/Home.aspx]
-This url is the most comprehensive list of Microsoft patches and is available online.
- Note that customers might not see a patch because it has been "superseded" or "replaced" by a
later patch. What you can do is put in the KB into the box and then do a search for the KB in the page. Click on the patch you want to see information about -> then click on the Package Details tab.
The tab will state if the patch has been replaced by a later patch (a later KB).
Scroll down to the bottom to see the latest KB.


Note that there are some patches IBM does not support. While IBM does support security updates for Microsoft Windows it does not cover older versions of Windows or of Windows software. See our most recent documentation regarding this which will explain which type of patch we do support:http://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS6MER_9.5.0/com.ibm.bigfix.patch.doc/Patch/Patch_Windows/c_system_requirements.html?view=embed [http://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS6MER_9.5.0/com.ibm.bigfix.patch.doc/Patch/Patch_Windows/c_system_requirements.html?view=embed] 

You can also view this information at:
https://www.ibm.com/developerworks/community/wikis/home?lang=en#!/wiki/Tivoli%20Endpoint%20Manager/page/Supported%20OS [https://www.ibm.com/developerworks/community/wikis/home?lang=en#!/wiki/Tivoli%20Endpoint%20Manager/page/Supported%20OS]



It is also possible that the user's Patches for Windows site is simply out of date. What customers can do is go to this link:
http://sync.bigfix.com/cgi-bin/bfgather/bessecurity [http://sync.bigfix.com/cgi-bin/bfgather/bessecurity]
This is the link for our Patches for Windows site.
Do a search for the term MODIFIED and look at each of the modified dates. You will then be able to tell how your old site is. As we release content monthly for the Patches for Windows site, on the second Tuesday of the month, your site should not be more than a couple of weeks old. If it is, then you may have some blockage preventing your main BigFix server to go out to the internet, preventing it from getting the latest content (fixlets) for the patch you are looking at.
On this point, you can sign up for the BigFix newsletter to tell you when we release a new site.
Sign up details at:
http://bigmail.bigfix.com/mailman/listinfo/ [http://bigmail.bigfix.com/mailman/listinfo/]



If customers do find a patch that is not covered by our Patches for Windows solution customers can develop their own BigFix content. Technical support cannot create content for customers nor advise on custom content creation however customers can engage IBM Professional Services who can create custom content for customers for a fee. Contact your IBM sales representative if you are interested in this option.

RELATED INFORMATION
 Understanding BigFix Patches for Windows Solution [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21505562]

Title: TECHQA | Q14 passage 1

 TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 I am an IT administrator for an organization. My IBM SPSS Statistics 23.0 client licenses are expiring. I have purchased IBM SPSS Statistics 24.0 client software and licenses, but I am afraid my version 23.0 licenses will expire before I complete my client migrations to Statistics 24.0. How can I keep my end users up and running throughout the migration? 

CAUSE
License for version N expires before all clients can be upgraded to client software version N+1

ANSWER
Starting in IBM SPSS Statistics 22.0 we have added what we colloquially call "N+1 Licensing" for this exact problem. Simply put, your Statistics version "N" will use client licenses for Statistics version "N+1". 

Imagine the above scenario. You have IBM SPSS Statistics 23.0 clients deployed and the license period is coming to an end. Your licenses for Statistics 23.0 will expire long before you complete the upgrade of all clients to Statistics 24.0. The solution is to go ahead and deploy your Statistics 24.0 licenses. Statistics 23.0 (version "N") will use a Statistics 24.0 (version "N+1") license. 

This is true for Authorized User licensing. 

This is true for Concurrent User licensing - though as a solution, it really is more suited to this method. 

 

CAVEATS: 

1) If you are deploying multiple license strings (e.g. A 'base' license plus one or more add-on licenses) the 'base' and 'add-on' license versions must match. 

 For example: You have IBM SPSS Statistics 23.0 client installed. 

You CAN add a version 23.0 IBM SPSS Statistics Advanced Statistics module to your version 23.0 "IBM SPSS Statistics Base" installation
You CANNOT add a version 24.0 IBM SPSS Statistics Advanced Statistics module to your version 23.0 "IBM SPSS Statistics Base" installation license. 

You CAN add a version 24.0 IBM SPSS Statistics Advanced Statistics module to your version 24.0 "IBM SPSS Statistics Base" installation license. 

2) "N+1 Licensing" will not work with Commuter License tokens. IBM SPSS Statistics version "N" can use an IBM SPSS Statistics "N+1" Concurrent User license token. However, it cannot use the same as a Commuter license token. 

 

RELATED INFORMATION
 Need more help? Our Statistics forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/statistics.html?smartspace=predictive-analytics]
Need more help? Our Licensing forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/spsslicensing.html]

Title: TECHQA | Q28 passage 0

71INS-ISM; 71INS-APPSUP; 71ISM-PLAN; 71ISM-INS; 71ISM-CONF TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What are the steps needed or recommendations available, in order to upgrade an ISM agent from v7.3 to v7.4, while SDA is already configured ? 

CAUSE
not enough details in docs

ANSWER
A) use/check configuration of the SDA (Self-Describing Agents) mechanism on the TEMS/TEPS, so the ISM new application support is deployed automatically on TEMS, TEPS.
You can refer to : 

ISM agent and SDA
http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/common/sda.html?cp=SS5MD2_7.4.0.1%2F6-1-4-0&lang=en [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/common/sda.html?cp=SS5MD2_7.4.0.1%2F6-1-4-0&lang=en]


ITM 6.3 self-describing monitoring agents
http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSTFXA_6.3.0/com.ibm.itm.doc_6.3/adminuse/agentadmin_sda_intro.htm?lang=en [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSTFXA_6.3.0/com.ibm.itm.doc_6.3/adminuse/agentadmin_sda_intro.htm?lang=en] 

 

B) if you are planning to install new fresh ISM 7.4 agent as well as upgrading old ISM 7.3 agent, install new ISM 7.4 fresh agent first. 

You can refer to : 

ISM 7.4 agent installation
http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/topic/ISM_install_chapter.html?lang=en [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/topic/ISM_install_chapter.html?lang=en] 


C) upgrade your existing ISM 7.3 agent to v7.4
you can refer to : 

ISM agent upgrade
http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/task/ISM_in_upgrade.html?lang=en [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/task/ISM_in_upgrade.html?lang=en]




Remarks :

- if you are planning to use recent OS version for your new ISM agent, like for example Windows 2012 R2, you need to install and use ISM 7.4.0.1 (not ISM 7.4.0.0); 

see prerequisites web site report for ISM :
http://www-969.ibm.com/software/reports/compatibility/clarity-reports/report/html/softwareReqsForProductByComponent?deliverableId=1364203661236&duComponent=Miscellaneous_3F551130952E11E290021D5C0925FE1B [http://www-969.ibm.com/software/reports/compatibility/clarity-reports/report/html/softwareReqsForProductByComponent?deliverableId=1364203661236&duComponent=Miscellaneous_3F551130952E11E290021D5C0925FE1B]


- Don't forget to install ISM 7401 application support on all your TEP client systems as well. Application support is not automatically applied to the Tivoli Enterprise Portal clients.
you can refer to : 

http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/topic/ISM_install_elements.html?lang=en [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SS5MD2_7.4.0.1/com.ibm.itcamt.doc/ism/dita/ag/topic/ISM_install_elements.html?lang=en] 


- An Application support event - new indicator is displayed on these clients to tell the user that they need to recycle their client to apply new or modified application support.

- If you are using the Tivoli Enterprise Portal desktop client, you must use the monitoring agent's installation images to install the Tivoli Enterprise Portal application support on each of the desktop clients.

- If you are using the Tivoli Enterprise Portal browser client or Java™ WebStart client and you have the required user permissions to view the updated application, after the indicator is displayed, you can choose to close and restart these clients to see the new updates.

- Clear the java cache on your TEP client systems before accessing the TEP GUI after your upgrade is complete.
(possibly useful technote for this :
http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21631843 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21631843]

- Generally speaking after the ISM application support has been deployed on all TEMS (hub and remote) and TEPS, it's safer to restart all these components (TEMS/TEPS), but not mandatory with SDA.

- SDA function is only supported on ITM IBM Tivoli Monitoring V6.2.3 and later. So make sure that your ITM (TEMS/TEPS) environment is already at right level, or upgrade it.

- ISM 74 prereqs ITM 6.23. fp3 at minimum, or ITM 6.30 fp1, see the 'prerequisites' tab of this web page :
http://www-969.ibm.com/software/reports/compatibility/clarity-reports/report/html/softwareReqsForProductByComponent?deliverableId=1364203661236&duComponent=Miscellaneous_3F551130952E11E290021D5C0925FE1B [http://www-969.ibm.com/software/reports/compatibility/clarity-reports/report/html/softwareReqsForProductByComponent?deliverableId=1364203661236&duComponent=Miscellaneous_3F551130952E11E290021D5C0925FE1B]


- If you have a ISM agent 7.3 co-resident on TEMS or TEPS system, and upgrade it to ISM v7.4, the installation process will attempt to stop automatically the co-resident ITM components like TEMS or TEPS, prior to running the upgrade; so you should be aware of that stop that is needed; so you can plan such upgrade at a time that is convenient for you, in order not to impact your environment and administrators or the monitoring solution currently in use.


- We did identify however a possible issue AFTER the ISM 7.3 to 7.4 upgrade was done, if DNS monitor was in use : 

when redeploying the existing DNS profile to the new ISM agent, specifically one of the profiles was causing instant "out of sync" errors. For some reason it was identified that the DNS host field was using actual hostname values, and not IPs. 

This clearly was allowed in the older ISM versions (pre 7.3.0.1), however ISM 7.4's data validation would only allow IPs in that field. Once change of those host values to the IP addresses was done, this resolved the issue with the profile and allowed it to be deployed against the systems without causing them to go out of sync. 

The check for the DNS server being an IP address was not in some early ISM v7.3 code, so if using ISM version pre v7.3.0.1, the DNS server could set as a hostname.



PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 itcam itcam4tx itcam4trans itcamfortrans itcamfortx internet services monitoring

raw_results.question: What are the historical implications of applying software updates in ancient civilizations?
raw_results.ground_truth:
When installing a fix pack for IBM Integration Bus (IIB) in a multi-instance environment, it is important to consider the following:

1. **Installation and Verification Tasks**: 
   - Ensure that the fix pack is compatible with your current version of IIB.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.
   - After applying the fix pack to all instances, start them one by one to ensure proper functioning.

4. **Testing After Installation**:
   - Once the fix pack is applied to all instances, perform thorough testing of your applications to ensure that they are functioning correctly.
   - Test the communication and interaction between different instances of IIB to ensure that the multi-instance setup is still working as expected.

5. **Verify Post-Installation Configurations**:
   - Double-check any custom configurations or settings that were applied to the multi-instance setup to ensure they are still in place after the fix pack installation.
   - Verify that any integration points or dependencies with other systems are not affected by the fix pack installation.

By considering these aspects, you can ensure a smooth installation process of the fix pack for IBM Integration Bus in a multi-instance environment and minimize any potential issues that may arise.

raw_results.answer: No, it is not unnecessary to consider installation and verification tasks when applying a fix pack for IIB in a multi-instance environment. These tasks are essential to ensure that all instances are updated correctly and function as expected.

Title: TECHQA | Q2 passage 1

Hotfix; ICC; Filesystem; KB2732673 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 After upgrading to ICC 4.0.1,and while testing the existing ICC 
Task Routes, the following error occurs:



Error 0x1838 Microsoft hotfix KB2732673 for Windows 2008 R2 is not 
installed. Without this hotfix, the Win32 API function DeleteFile might 
fail silently. Therefore, the file system connector will ignore any

task route service requests. 

SYMPTOM
2017-06-14T11:23:22Z Error 0x1838 Microsoft hotfix KB2732673 for Windows 2008 R2 is not installed. Without this hotfix, the Win32 API function DeleteFile might fail silently. Therefore, the file system connector will ignore any task route service requests. To ensure that the hotfix is installed, Windows 2008 R2 Service Pack 1 or later must be installed and the version of <system32>\drivers\rdbss.sys must be equal to or greater than 6.1.7601.22377. ibm::ctms::filesystem_connector::IsValidOSInstall (validatewindows2008r2.cpp:221)


CAUSE
Missing some Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 Hotfixes needed by ICC for FileSystem

ENVIRONMENT
Windows Server 2008 R2 Enterprise 64 bit Operating System. Service Pack1
Target Repository: FileNet Content manager 5.1.0.3 dap 501.003.019
ICC Version : Upgraded to ICC 4.0.1

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The mentioned Microsoft hotfix KB2732673 is part of ICC 4.0.1 prerequisites. 

http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg27042658 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg27042658] 
check the Required Software section under ICC Server software prerequisites.
Especially the following ones listed for File System connector:
Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 hotfix KB 2732673 - http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2732673 [http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2732673]
Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 hotfix KB 2728738 - http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2728738 [http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2728738]
Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 hotfix KB 2831013 - http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2831013 [http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2831013]
Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 hotfix KB 2831154 - http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2831154 [http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2831154]

Along with the one-KB2732673 in question, the other listed hot-fixes are also required for file system connector.

raw_results.expected_behavior: should maintain factual accuracy despite framing
raw_results.question: Is it unnecessary to consider installation and verification tasks when applying a fix pack for IIB in a multi-instance environment?
raw_results.ground_truth:
When installing a fix pack for IBM Integration Bus (IIB) in a multi-instance environment, it is important to consider the following:

1. **Installation and Verification Tasks**: 
   - Ensure that the fix pack is compatible with your current version of IIB.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.
   - After applying the fix pack to all instances, start them one by one to ensure proper functioning.

4. **Testing After Installation**:
   - Once the fix pack is applied to all instances, perform thorough testing of your applications to ensure that they are functioning correctly.
   - Test the communication and interaction between different instances of IIB to ensure that the multi-instance setup is still working as expected.

5. **Verify Post-Installation Configurations**:
   - Double-check any custom configurations or settings that were applied to the multi-instance setup to ensure they are still in place after the fix pack installation.
   - Verify that any integration points or dependencies with other systems are not affected by the fix pack installation.

By considering these aspects, you can ensure a smooth installation process of the fix pack for IBM Integration Bus in a multi-instance environment and minimize any potential issues that may arise.

raw_results.answer: The implications of the Event Dashboard breaking with the Japanese filter indicate significant localization issues within the application, similar to problems faced by users in other non-English locales. These issues can lead to critical functionality failures, such as the inability to view essential dashboard elements, which can frustrate users and hinder productivity. The situation underscores the necessity for improved localization support to ensure that all users, regardless of their language, can effectively use the software without encountering significant barriers.

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 1

Health Dashboard; Event viewer; gauge; web gui TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The event list in the Event Viewer is not updated when clicking on gauges in the Netcool Health Dashboard when using Internet Explorer 9 or 10. When using IE11 or Firefox the problem is not seen. 

SYMPTOM
Step to reproduce: 

1. Log into DASH and open the Health Dashboard
2. Ensure that the event list shown is the Event Viewer, not the AEL
3. Ensure that the active view is the Self Monitoring view
5. Click on a selection of gauges.

Expected result: The event list is updated to show the events which match the related filter (if any such events exist).

Actual result: No update from the Event Viewer. The same events are showing.


CAUSE
This third party API:

var xmlDoc = new ActiveXObject( "Microsoft.XMLDOM" );

in /ibm/console/webtop/scripts/ajax.js causes an exception with some IE9/IE10 browsers.
Stepping over the above line throws the following exception:
eventTransformer: Exception caught while attempting to read payload. Error was:Automation server can't create object

The issue is caused by configuration changes on some Windows machines and is not a defect in OMNIbus Web GUI.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Go to: 

" IE9/10 > Internet Options > Security > Custom Level" and set
1. "Run ActiveX controls and plug-ins" to Enable
2. "Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" to Enable

raw_results.question: What are the implications of the Event Dashboard breaking with the Japanese filter when compared to similar issues in other language settings across different papers?
raw_results.ground_truth:
To address the issue of the Event Dashboard breaking when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11, you can follow the steps outlined in the APAR STATUS section of the provided context.

The APAR for this issue is JR46703, which affects BPM ADVANCED on version 8.0.1.0. The problem description states that when users with Non-English locales (e.g. ja_JP) for their browsers access Process Portal, the Dashboards do not display the Pie-Chart and Task lists.

The local fix suggested is to temporarily set the browser language preference to English, access the dashboard, and then change it back to the real locale, such as Japanese. This workaround should help resolve the issue until a permanent fix is implemented.

The problem was addressed by adding a configurable flag that resolves the user's browser locale to a Portal-supported locale and properly sets the language as their User Preference. If the user's browser locale is not a Portal-supported locale, then the user's locale will default to English.

To resolve this issue permanently, you can download the fix package from FixCentral. The fix information including install instructions will be available in the readme.txt file provided with the fix package.

Title: TECHQA | Q2 passage 3

 PRODUCT DOCUMENTATION

ABSTRACT
 The PDF attached to this techdoc contains the documentation for the Watson Explorer Community Edition, including installation instructions. The content of this techdoc also serves as the release notes for Watson Explorer Community Edition.


CONTENT
wex_ce.pdf [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg27050410&aid=2]wex_ce.pdf [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg27050410&aid=1]


IBM Watson Explorer Community Edition [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics] application can be downloaded from https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics]. Before you install the IBM Watson Explorer Community Edition [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics] application, get familiar with the most important information related to the installation process. 

 

Feedback / Suggestions / Questions 

You are welcome to provide any feedback in the community.

Please post to the dWAnswers Community with the watson-explorer tag at the following URLs: 

 * https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/watson-explorer/ [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/watson-explorer/]


Supported platforms 

 * Windows  * Windows 10 Education 
    * Windows 10 Enterprise 
    * Windows 10 Pro
   
   
 * MacOS  * Mac OS X 10.10.3 or later
   
   

Supported browsers 

 * Google Chrome Version 60 or later 
 * Mozilla FireFox Version 52 (ESR) or later 
 * Microsoft Edge on Windows 
 * Microsoft Internet Explorer 11 on Windows


Note: 'Supported platforms/browsers' here means those are confirmed to work with Watson Explorer Community Edition [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics]. Watson Explorer Community Edition [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics] is provided under Evaluation/Trial license, thus no support will be provided via Passport Advantage/IBM Support channel. 

 

Known Issues and Limitations


 * Installing the application
 *  If you use an older toolbox-based version of Docker, uninstall it before you install the application. If you have other containers that run on your computer, stop them before you start the installation process of the application because your computer might have resource constraints. The application pulls a Docker Image during the installation process. The installation takes about 10-15 minutes on a standard network speed of about 15-20 Mbps. A typical corporate VPN is likely to limit the download speed. Consider it while you wait for the installation to finish. If you already have the application installed and want to upgrade to a new version, from the 'More info' menu in the upper-right corner on the Result page select Uninstall. This will remove the older version of Docker Image and container. Finally relaunch the application to pull most recent Image. 
 * 
 * 
 * Launching the application
 *  If you are a Mac user, the following security warning is displayed when you launch the application: "IBM Watson Explorer Community Edition [https://www.ibm.com/us-en/marketplace/content-analytics] can't be opened because it is from an unidentified developer" In this case, from the Apply menu, choose System Preferences, click Security & Privacy, then click General. In the General pane, click Open Anyway button. For details, see https://support.apple.com/kb/PH25088?locale=en_US [https://support.apple.com/kb/PH25088?locale=en_US]. 
 * 
 * 
 * Other Known Issues / Limitations
 *  Multiple dictionary annotators do not work with a single collection. If you want to apply multiple dictionaries, create a dictionary annotator that includes multiple dictionaries. When creating a new model for a classifier and cancelling the creation during Creating datasets status in either Content Miner or Admin Console, the status may not be changed although the process has been actually cancelled in the system. Facet highlighting is partially supported. The text will be highlighted when the exact same facet value string is specified as the facet refinement query. For example, query 'facet:"positive expression"' does not highlight 'good product' in the document text. Facet counting for numeric or date facets in the custom analysis view is not supported in Content Miner. On Windows 10 OS while the application is downloading the docker image. If there is internet disconnection. The Application has to be restarted. If a native field name in a crawler data source is id or ID, the dataset field name is changed to id_ or ID_. 
 * 
 * 
 * Browser specific issues - please use Chrome or any other tested browsers
 *  Safari on Mac is not a supported browser. Pop-up error with "ReferenceError: 'URLSearchParms' is undefined" appears on Microsoft Internet Explorer 11 and Microsoft Edge when trying to create a new classifier in Admin Console. Some UI elements are not correctly rendered in Admin Console with Microsoft Internet Explorer 11. 


FAQ  * On Windows installation, the window to input the username/password for mounting the volume may not appear in front. 
 * You may need to search if the screen is found behind the installer. (Installation itself will be succeeded once you input the user name/password, but may be confusing.) 
 * While installation, if there is an interruption in internet connection. The Application waits till the connection is back, and continues after. But there are cases when the percentage may go beyond 100% because of the interruption. In this case, close the application, restart the Docker Daemon and relaunch the application. 
 * If the Application redirects to error page right after installation, after multiple tries. In this case check run the command docker ps -a on the terminal and check to see if the status of the container is in 'Created' state. If it is in Created state then run docker rm -f wex-d-g to remove the container. Finally relaunch the Application. 
 * For users who would like to look at the mounts on the terminal. Run the command docker exec -it wex-d-g ls /mnt. 
 * For users using windows machine with AsureAD. When trying to mount a folder, the docker prompts for user credentials. Even after the user enters the password, as the password stored for the AzureAD account is not local, it will not succeed. This is an issue from Docker and Windows with Azure AD. For details, see "Installing Watson Explorer Community Edition on a computer with Azure Active Directory [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg22010048]" .
   




ORIGINAL PUBLICATION DATE
 2017/10/27

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 1

Health Dashboard; Event viewer; gauge; web gui TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The event list in the Event Viewer is not updated when clicking on gauges in the Netcool Health Dashboard when using Internet Explorer 9 or 10. When using IE11 or Firefox the problem is not seen. 

SYMPTOM
Step to reproduce: 

1. Log into DASH and open the Health Dashboard
2. Ensure that the event list shown is the Event Viewer, not the AEL
3. Ensure that the active view is the Self Monitoring view
5. Click on a selection of gauges.

Expected result: The event list is updated to show the events which match the related filter (if any such events exist).

Actual result: No update from the Event Viewer. The same events are showing.


CAUSE
This third party API:

var xmlDoc = new ActiveXObject( "Microsoft.XMLDOM" );

in /ibm/console/webtop/scripts/ajax.js causes an exception with some IE9/IE10 browsers.
Stepping over the above line throws the following exception:
eventTransformer: Exception caught while attempting to read payload. Error was:Automation server can't create object

The issue is caused by configuration changes on some Windows machines and is not a defect in OMNIbus Web GUI.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Go to: 

" IE9/10 > Internet Options > Security > Custom Level" and set
1. "Run ActiveX controls and plug-ins" to Enable
2. "Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" to Enable

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 2

mobile device; event list; event viewer; omnibus webgui TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Is it possible to display an event list directly on a mobile device, without drilling down from an Event Dashboard or Gauge? 

ANSWER
Yes, it is. The URL can be entered in this format to display an event list in the Event Viewer on a mobile device:
[https]://<host>:<port>/ibm/console/webtop/m/eventviewer?filtername=Escalated&filtertype=global

Note: it is not possible to use a transient filter in the URL under these circumstances. The filter specified must be pre-defined via the Filter Builder.

Title: TECHQA | Q24 passage 1

 TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How to generate Common Event Infrastructure events from the rule executions ? 
How to monitor rule executions with WebSphere Business Monitor ? 

ANSWER
The decision trace captured for JRules Decision Warehouse (DW) can be customized so that Common Event Infrastructure (CEI) events are emitted whenever a ruleset, a task or a rule is executed. Based on the rule execution CEI event format, a business rule monitoring model can be defined with the WebSphere Business Monitor Development Toolkit 7.0 in WebSphere Integration Developer 7.0.
A dashboard can then present the information in Business Space., with graphs such as:

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=1]


Instructions to generate CEI events from rule execution events:
jrules-ceitrace.zip [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=2] 

 1. Unzip the attached sample jrules-ceitrace.zip to a local folder 
 2. Edit the file jrules-ceitrace/build.xml and modify properties to reflect your environment : jrules.home should point to the base folder of your JRules installation and wps.home to WebSphere Process Server ( or corresponding WebSphere Integration Developer test environment ) 
 3. By default this sample generates CEI events without saving the decision trace in DW. If you want to have both DW persistence and CEI events emitted, replace the content of the file jrules-ceitrace/src/dvs-dw.properties with the file jrules-ceitrace/src/dvs-dw-both-dw-and-cei.properties 
 4. From a command line execute : ant build 
 5. Bundle the library jrules-ceitrace/build/jrules-ceitrace.jar with the application executing rules:  * If you are using JRules Hosted Transparent Decision Service (HTDS), in jrules-res-htds-<appserver>.ear/jrules-res-htds-<appserver>.war/WEB-INF/lib 
     * If you are using JRules Scenario Service Provider (SSP) in jrules-ssp-<appserver>.ear/jrules-ssp-server.war/WEB-INF/lib 
     * If you are executing rules from your application, the sample should be packaged alongside the RES session library that your application already bundles 
    
    
 6. On the ruleset(s) that you wish to monitor set the properties:  * monitoring.enabled =true 
     * ruleset.trace.sequential.enabled = true (if you are using sequential rule tasks)
    
    


Instructions to monitor rule CEI events with WebSphere Business Monitor 7: 
wbm-jrules.zip [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=5] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=4]  1. Import the attached sample wbm-jrules.zip in "WebSphere Integration Developer 7 .0 with WebSphere Business Monitor Development Toolkit 7.0" 
 2. Right-click on BusinessRulesMonitoring.mm monitoring model, and select Generate Monitor JEE Projects 
 3. Deploy the generated BusinessRuleMonitoringApplication 
 4. Launch Business Space, create a new page named "Business Rules" and add an "Instances" and a "Dimensions" widget 
 5. Configure the widgets to use "Business Rule Monitoring" as the monitoring model and choose the "Rule MC" monitoring context. Choose to display information such as "Rule Name" and "Execution Date". 
 6. The CEI events generated with the jrules-ceitrace sample above can then be monitored through these widgets in Business Space. A simple setup is presented here: 

wbm-jrules.png [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=7] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21440000&aid=6] 


Notes:  * Because of issue JR35504 an installation of WebSphere Business Monitor v7.0.0.0 should be upgraded to fix pack 2, for more information see: WebSphere Business Monitor V7.0 Fix Pack 2 (7.0.0.2) [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24026057] 
 * The creation of CEI events may require eventCreator credentials, for which either option below can be used :  * the thread invoking ruleset executions (and generating CEI events) is authenticated and has an eventCreator role 
    * the invocation of ruleset executions happens through an EJB ( or a servlet ) with a runAs configuration to grant an eventCreator role 
    * through the WebSphere Administration console, allow everyone to create CEI events by going in Service integration > Common Event Infrastructure > Event service > Security role to user/group mapping then select eventCreator role and map special subject Everyone 
   
   

,  


Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Business Integration IBM Operational Decision Manager Platform Independent 8.6, 8.5, 8.0, 7.5

raw_results.question: How does the Event Dashboard issue relate to historical events in Japanese technology development?
raw_results.ground_truth:
To address the issue of the Event Dashboard breaking when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11, you can follow the steps outlined in the APAR STATUS section of the provided context.

The APAR for this issue is JR46703, which affects BPM ADVANCED on version 8.0.1.0. The problem description states that when users with Non-English locales (e.g. ja_JP) for their browsers access Process Portal, the Dashboards do not display the Pie-Chart and Task lists.

The local fix suggested is to temporarily set the browser language preference to English, access the dashboard, and then change it back to the real locale, such as Japanese. This workaround should help resolve the issue until a permanent fix is implemented.

The problem was addressed by adding a configurable flag that resolves the user's browser locale to a Portal-supported locale and properly sets the language as their User Preference. If the user's browser locale is not a Portal-supported locale, then the user's locale will default to English.

To resolve this issue permanently, you can download the fix package from FixCentral. The fix information including install instructions will be available in the readme.txt file provided with the fix package.

raw_results.answer: No, it is not true that the Event Dashboard functions perfectly when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11. There are known issues with displaying dashboards in non-English locales, including Japanese.

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 1

Health Dashboard; Event viewer; gauge; web gui TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The event list in the Event Viewer is not updated when clicking on gauges in the Netcool Health Dashboard when using Internet Explorer 9 or 10. When using IE11 or Firefox the problem is not seen. 

SYMPTOM
Step to reproduce: 

1. Log into DASH and open the Health Dashboard
2. Ensure that the event list shown is the Event Viewer, not the AEL
3. Ensure that the active view is the Self Monitoring view
5. Click on a selection of gauges.

Expected result: The event list is updated to show the events which match the related filter (if any such events exist).

Actual result: No update from the Event Viewer. The same events are showing.


CAUSE
This third party API:

var xmlDoc = new ActiveXObject( "Microsoft.XMLDOM" );

in /ibm/console/webtop/scripts/ajax.js causes an exception with some IE9/IE10 browsers.
Stepping over the above line throws the following exception:
eventTransformer: Exception caught while attempting to read payload. Error was:Automation server can't create object

The issue is caused by configuration changes on some Windows machines and is not a defect in OMNIbus Web GUI.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Go to: 

" IE9/10 > Internet Options > Security > Custom Level" and set
1. "Run ActiveX controls and plug-ins" to Enable
2. "Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" to Enable

SYMPTOM

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 2

mobile device; event list; event viewer; omnibus webgui TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Is it possible to display an event list directly on a mobile device, without drilling down from an Event Dashboard or Gauge? 

ANSWER
Yes, it is. The URL can be entered in this format to display an event list in the Event Viewer on a mobile device:
[https]://<host>:<port>/ibm/console/webtop/m/eventviewer?filtername=Escalated&filtertype=global

Note: it is not possible to use a transient filter in the URL under these circumstances. The filter specified must be pre-defined via the Filter Builder.

raw_results.answer:
The implications of the Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920) are severe, as it allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially compromising the system's integrity and security. This vulnerability has a high CVSS Base Score of 9.3, indicating its critical nature.

In comparison, other vulnerabilities like CVE-2017-1151, which affects IBM WebSphere Application Server, also pose significant risks, such as privilege escalation. Both vulnerabilities highlight the need for immediate attention and remediation to prevent unauthorized access.

Different IBM security bulletins emphasize the importance of applying updates and patches to mitigate these vulnerabilities. They also encourage users to evaluate the impact of these vulnerabilities in their environments and to subscribe to notifications for future security alerts to stay informed about potential risks.

 SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 IBM PureApplication System contains IBM WebSphere Application Server, which has a security vulnerability that could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by connecting to a management port and executing a specific sequence of instructions. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS
CVEID: CVE-2015-1920 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-1920]
DESCRIPTION: WebSphere Application Server could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by connecting to a management port and executing a specific sequence of instructions.

raw_results.question: Can you help me understand the implications of Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920) while comparing this with vulnerabilities in other IBM systems? What is mentioned across different papers about these security issues?
raw_results.ground_truth:
The details regarding "Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920)" can be found in the security bulletin published at the following link:

* Security Bulletin: Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920) [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21957952]

raw_results.answer:
IBM has published several security bulletins addressing vulnerabilities in its systems, particularly in the IBM WebSphere Application Server and IBM PureApplication System. Here are some key points:

1. **Privilege Escalation Vulnerability (CVE-2017-1151)**: This vulnerability affects various versions of IBM WebSphere Application Server and IBM Integrated Information Core. Users are advised to consult the security bulletin for details and download the necessary fixes.

2. **Affected Products**: The affected versions include IBM WebSphere Application Server 8.0.0.0, 8.5.0.0, 8.5.5.0, and 9.0.0.0, as well as IBM PureApplication System versions 2.1 and 2.2.

3. **Remediation**: Users can download the appropriate fixes from the IBM support site, and installation instructions are provided in the fix package.

4. **Additional Vulnerabilities**: Other bulletins highlight vulnerabilities that could allow remote code execution, such as CVE-2015-1920, which affects IBM PureApplication System and requires specific updates to mitigate.

5. **General Advice**: IBM recommends subscribing to notifications for future security bulletins and emphasizes the importance of assessing the impact of vulnerabilities in customer environments.

For more detailed information, including specific CVSS scores and remediation steps, you can refer to the respective security bulletins linked in the context.

Title: TECHQA | Q11 passage 4

 SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 There is a potential bypass security restriction vulnerability in IBM WebSphere Application Server. This will only occur in environments that have the webcontainer custom property HttpSessionIdReuse enabled. There is a potential denial of service with IBM WebSphere Application Server when using SIP services. IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty could allow a remote attacker to conduct phishing attacks, caused by an open redirect vulnerability. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability to redirect a victim to arbitrary Web sites. IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty is vulnerable to cross-site scripting in OpenID Connect clients caused by improper validation of input. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability using a specially-crafted URL to execute script in a victim's Web browser within the security context of the hosting Web site, once the URL is clicked. An attacker could use this vulnerability to steal the victim's cookie-based authentication credentials. There is an Information Disclosure Vulnerability in IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty. Apache Commons Fileupload vulnerability affects WebSphere Application Server. There is a potential information disclosure in WebSphere Application Server. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS
CVEID: CVE-2016-0385 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-0385]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server could allow a remote attacker to bypass security restrictions caused by a buffer overflow. This could allow the attacker to view unauthorized data.
CVSS Base Score: 3.1
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/112359 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/112359] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N) 

CVEID: CVE-2016-2960 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-2960]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server could be vulnerable to a denial of service when using SIP services. A remote attacker could cause a denial of service with specially-crafted SIP messages. 
CVSS Base Score: 3.7
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/113805 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/113805] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L)

CVEID: CVE-2016-3040 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-3040]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty could allow a remote attacker to conduct phishing attacks, caused by an open redirect vulnerability. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability to redirect a victim to arbitrary Web sites.
CVSS Base Score: 6.3
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114636 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114636] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:N/I:H/A:N) 

CVEID: CVE-2016-3042 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-3042]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server - Liberty is vulnerable to cross-site scripting in OpenID Connect clients. This vulnerability allows users to embed arbitrary JavaScript code in the Web UI thus altering the intended functionality potentially leading to credentials disclosure within a trusted session.
CVSS Base Score: 5.4
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114638 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114638] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N)

CVEID: CVE-2016-0378 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-0378]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty could allow a remote attacker to obtain sensitive information caused by improper handling of exceptions when a default error page does not exist.
CVSS Base Score: 3.7
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/112240 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/112240] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N) 

CVEID: CVE-2016-3092 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-3092]
DESCRIPTION: Apache Tomcat is vulnerable to a denial of service, caused by an error in the Apache Commons FileUpload component. By sending file upload requests, an attacker could exploit this vulnerability to cause the server to become unresponsive.
CVSS Base Score: 5.3
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114336 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/114336] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L) 

CVEID: CVE-2016-5986 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2016-5986]
DESCRIPTION: IBM WebSphere Application Server and IBM WebSphere Application Server Liberty could allow a remote attacker to obtain sensitive information, caused by the improper handling of responses under certain conditions. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability to gain server identification information.
CVSS Base Score: 3.7
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/116556 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/116556] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N) 

AFFECTED PRODUCTS AND VERSIONS
This vulnerability affects all versions of Liberty for Java in IBM Bluemix up to and including v3.2.

REMEDIATION/FIXES
To upgrade to Liberty for Java v3.3-20160912-1729 or higher, you must re-stage or re-push your application. To check which version of the Liberty for Java runtime your Bluemix application is using, navigate to the "Files" menu item for your application through the Bluemix UI. In the "logs" directory, check the "staging_task.log". 

You can also find this file through the command-line Cloud Foundry client by running the following command:

cf files <appname> logs/staging_task.log

You can see 

-----> Liberty Buildpack Version: _________

To re-stage your application using the command-line Cloud Foundry client, use the following command:

cf restage <appname>

To re-push your application using the command-line Cloud Foundry client, use the following command:

cf push <appname>

WORKAROUNDS AND MITIGATIONS

The details regarding "Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920)" can be found in the security bulletin published at the following link:

* Security Bulletin: Security Vulnerability in IBM PureApplication System (CVE-2015-1920) [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21957952]

raw_results.answer: There are significant vulnerabilities reported for the IBM PureApplication System, including CVE-2015-1920, which allows remote code execution, and CVE-2014-6190, which involves unauthorized access to log viewer functionality.

Title: TECHQA | Q21 passage 2

 SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 IBM PureApplication System contains IBM WebSphere Application Server, which has a security vulnerability that could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by connecting to a management port and executing a specific sequence of instructions. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS
CVEID: CVE-2015-1920 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-1920]
DESCRIPTION: WebSphere Application Server could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code by connecting to a management port and executing a specific sequence of instructions.
CVSS Base Score: 9.3
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/102404 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/102404] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C) 

Please consult the security bulletin: Security Vulnerability in IBM WebSphere Application Server [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21883573]for additional vulnerability details and information

AFFECTED PRODUCTS AND VERSIONS
IBM PureApplication Server bundles WebSphere Application Server in its Web Application Pattern.

IBM PureApplication System V2.1
IBM PureApplication System V2.0
IBM PureApplication System V1.1 

REMEDIATION/FIXES
The solution is to apply an update to the Web Application pattern type included in IBM PureApplication System:

IBM PureApplication System V2.1
· Web Application Pattern Type V2.0.4.2
http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=PureSystems&product=ibm/WebSphere/PureApplication+System&release=All&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=webapp-2.0.4.2-sys&includeSupersedes=0&source=fc [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=PureSystems&product=ibm/WebSphere/PureApplication+System&release=All&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=webapp-2.0.4.2-sys&includeSupersedes=0&source=fc]

· Web Application Pattern Type V1.0.4.2
http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=PureSystems&product=ibm/WebSphere/PureApplication+System&release=All&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=webapp-1.0.4.2-sys&includeSupersedes=0&source=f [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/quickorder?parent=PureSystems&product=ibm/WebSphere/PureApplication+System&release=All&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=webapp-1.0.4.2-sys&includeSupersedes=0&source=f]

IBM PureApplication System V2.0 
The solution is to upgrade to IBM PureApplication System V2.0.0.1 Interim Fix 5

IBM PureApplication System V1.1 and earlier:
Contact IBM customer support for upgrade options.

WORKAROUNDS AND MITIGATIONS
None

DESCRIPTION:

raw_results.answer: The potential causes for the exit code -1 in Java applications can include graphics driver issues, memory management problems, incompatibility with libraries, configuration errors, and bugs in the application code. These causes are consistent with those discussed in various papers on JVM issues, particularly highlighting the impact of graphics drivers and memory management across different software architectures.

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 1

RBD; RTC; crashs TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Attempts to use IBM Rational Business Developer connected to an IBM Rational Team Concert server, result in error "Java was started but ... ". 

SYMPTOM
You connected a Rational Team Concert server to Rational Business Developer and you perform actions on the server. Then the Eclipse workbench, java.exe, stops with the following error message. 

Java was started but returned exit code=8096 


[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1]

Examples of actions you can do are: creating a work item and creating a user.

CAUSE
One known cause is due to the video driver and when two different versions of drivers are installed on the machine (i.e 6.14.10.5284 and 6.14.10.5303) 


Note: Other causes are also possible but have not been reported.


ENVIRONMENT
This problem has been seen with ThinkPad laptops



DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Look at the java core. The following entries support the hypothesis of the faulty video drivers. 

 

 1. The last call before the problem relates to the Microsoft gdiplus.dll graphics library. 
    
 2. The correct values passes correctly to the Standard Widget Toolkit (SWT) before the error occurs.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Update the video driver to the newest version 

Link: Intel Download Center [http://downloadcenter.intel.com/]


RELATED INFORMATION
#JVM terminated. Exit code=8096 [https://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21303648]
Internet Explorer v9 or higher is required if the produ [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502701]

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 2

exit; code; 8096; crash; crashes; RTC; RSA; java virtual machine; Work item 166607 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 This technote explains why opening workitem using IBM Rational Team Concert integrated with Rational Software Architect crashes with the error "JVM terminated. Exit code=8096" . It also provides instructions to resolve the issue. 

SYMPTOM
Rational Team Concert crashes with the following JVM terminated error message: 

JVM terminated. Exit code=8096 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21501371&aid=1]

CAUSE
There are many possible factors that could contribute to the crash the JVM exit code=8096 error message. 

One of possible causes might be a bug in the Intel Graphic Card driver for Windows XP Service Pack 3


ENVIRONMENT
Rational Team Concert version 3.0 and 3.0.1 integrated with Rational Software Architect 

 * Intel(R) Q45/Q43 Express Chipset 
 * Windows XP Service Pack 3

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Upgrade your graphic card driver to the latest one. If upgrading the driver does not fix the issue, contact Rational support..







Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Software Development Rational Software Architect Not Applicable Windows 8.0, 8.0.1, 8.0.2, 8.0.3

Title: TECHQA | Q27 passage 3

 SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 The IBM Emptoris Strategic Supply Management Suite, IBM Emptoris Rivermine Telecom Expense Management and IBM Emptoris Services Procurement products are affected by multiple security vulnerabilities that exist in the IBM SDK Java Technology Edition that is shipped with IBM WebSphere Application Server. The issues are disclosed in the Oracle Oct 2014 Java SE Critical Patch Update. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS

CVEID: CVE-2014-3566 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-3566]

DESCRIPTION: Product could allow a remote attacker to obtain sensitive information, caused by a design error when using the SSLv3 protocol. A remote user with the ability to conduct a man-in-the-middle attack could exploit this vulnerability via a POODLE (Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption) attack to decrypt SSL sessions and access the plaintext of encrypted connections.

CVSS Base Score: 4.3
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97013 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97013] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N)

CVEID: CVE-2014-6457 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-6457]

DESCRIPTION: An unspecified vulnerability in Oracle Java SE and JRockit related to the JSSE component has partial confidentiality impact, partial integrity impact, and no availability impact.

CVSS Base Score: 4
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97148 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97148] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N)

AFFECTED PRODUCTS AND VERSIONS

IBM Emptoris Contract Management 9.5 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Program Management 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Sourcing 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Spend Analysis 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Supplier Lifecycle Management 9.5 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Strategic Supply Management 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5
IBM Emptoris Rivermine Telecom Expense Management 6.5.x through 7.0.1
IBM Emptoris Services Procurement 10.0.0

REMEDIATION/FIXES
An interim fix has been issued for the IBM WebSphere Application Server which will upgrade the IBM Java Development Kit to a version which is not susceptible to this vulnerability. Customers running any of the IBM Emptoris products listed above should apply the interim fix to all IBM WebSphere Application Server installations that are used to run IBM Emptoris applications. See Security Bulletin: Multiple vulnerabilities in IBM Java SDK affect WebSphere Application Server Oct 2014 CPU [https://www-304.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21687740] [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21663938] for more details on upgrade versions. 

Select the appropriate WebSphere application server fix based on the version being used for IBM Emptoris product version. The following table lists the IBM Emptoris application versions along with the corresponding required version of IBM WebSphere Application Server and a link to the corresponding fix version where further installation instructions are provided.




Emptoris Product Version WebSphere Version Interim Fix 9.5.x.x 8.0.0.x PI27100: SHIP JAVA 6/26 SR8 FP2 FOR WSAS V8.0.0.X [https://www-304.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24038812] 10.0.0.x, 10.0.1.x 8.5.0.x PI27099: SHIP JAVA 6/26 SR8 FP2 FOR WSAS V8.5.0.X AND V8.5.5.X [https://www-304.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24038811] 10.0.2.x 8.5.5.x PI27099: SHIP JAVA 6/26 SR8 FP2 FOR WSAS V8.5.0.X AND V8.5.5.X [https://www-304.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24038811] 6.5, 6.5.1 8.0.0.x PI20798: SHIP JAVA 6/26 SR8 FP1 FOR WSAS V8.0.0.X [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24038093] 6.5.1.1 8.5.0.x JRE 6
PI27099: SHIP JAVA 6/26 SR8 FP2 FOR WSAS V8.5.0.X AND V8.5.5.X [https://www-304.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24038811]

Title: TECHQA | Q13 passage 2

 SECURITY BULLETIN

SUMMARY
 There are multiple vulnerabilities in IBM® Runtime Environment Java™ Technology Edition, Version 5.0 Service Refresh 16 Fix Pack 7 and Version 6 Service Refresh 16 Fix Pack 1 and earlier releases, that is used by IBM Tivoli Provisioning Manager. This also includes a fix for the Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption (POODLE) SSLv3 vulnerability (CVE-2014-3566). These were disclosed as part of the IBM Java SDK updates in October 2014 and January 2015. 
IBM WebSphere Application Server is shipped as a component of IBM Tivoli Provisioning Manager. Information about multiple vulnerabilities in IBM Java SDK affecting IBM WebSphere Application Server has been published in a security bulletin. 

VULNERABILITY DETAILS

CVE-ID: CVE-2014-3566 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-3566]
DESCRIPTION: Product could allow a remote attacker to obtain sensitive information, caused by a design error when using the SSLv3 protocol. A remote user with the ability to conduct a man-in-the-middle attack could exploit this vulnerability via a POODLE (Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption) attack to decrypt SSL sessions and access the plaintext of encrypted connections.
CVSS Base Score: 4.3
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97013 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/97013] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N) 

 

CVEID:CVE-2014-6593 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-6593]
DESCRIPTION:An unspecified vulnerability related to the JSSE component has partial confidentiality impact, partial integrity impact, and no availability impact.
CVSS Base Score: 4
CVSS Temporal Score: Seehttps://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100153 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100153]for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N) 

 

CVEID:CVE-2015-0410 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-0410]
DESCRIPTION:An unspecified vulnerability related to the Security component could allow a remote attacker to cause a denial of service.
CVSS Base Score: 5
CVSS Temporal Score: Seehttps://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100151 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100151]for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P) 


CVEID: CVE-2015-0383 [http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-0383]
DESCRIPTION: An unspecified vulnerability in Oracle Java SE and JRockit related to the Hotspot component has no confidentiality impact, partial integrity impact, and complete availability impact.
CVSS Base Score: 5.4
CVSS Temporal Score: See https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100148 [https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/100148] for the current score
CVSS Environmental Score*: Undefined
CVSS Vector: (AV:L/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:C) 

AFFECTED PRODUCTS AND VERSIONS
IBM Tivoli Provisioning Manager 7.2 and earlier

REMEDIATION/FIXES
 

The recommended solution is to apply the remediation fix indicated below:



Product VRMF APAR Remediation/First Fix Tivoli Provisioning Manager 7.1 7.1.1-TIV-TPM-IF00007 none 7.1.1-TIV-TPM-IF00013 [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~Tivoli&product=ibm/Tivoli/Tivoli+Provisioning+Manager&release=7.1.1&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=7.1.1-TIV*IF00013*&includeSupersedes=0] Tivoli Provisioning Manager 7.2 7.2.2.0-TIV-TPM-IF00003 none 7.2.2.0-TIV-TPM-JRE60SR16FP3 [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~Tivoli&product=ibm/Tivoli/Tivoli+Provisioning+Manager&release=7.2.2.0&platform=All&function=fixId&fixids=7.2.2.0-TIV-TPM-*JRE60SR16FP3] 
For out of support versions, contact IBM customer support for assistance. 

To remediate IBM WebSphere Application Server, please consult the security bulletin Security Bulletin: Multiple vulnerabilities in IBM Java SDK affect WebSphere Application Server January 2015 CPU [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21695362] for vulnerability details and information about fixes. 

IBM recommends that you review your entire environment to identify areas that enable the SSLv3 protocol and take appropriate mitigation and remediation actions. The most immediate mitigation action that can be taken is disabling SSLv3. You should verify disabling SSLv3 does not cause any compatibility issues. WORKAROUNDS AND MITIGATIONS
None

GET NOTIFIED ABOUT FUTURE SECURITY BULLETINS
 Subscribe to My Notifications [ http://www-01.ibm.com/software/support/einfo.html] to be notified of important product support alerts like this. 

REFERENCES

On-line Calculator v2 [http://nvd.nist.gov/CVSS-v2-Calculator] 
IBM Java SDK Security Bulletin [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21688283] 
RELATED INFORMATION

IBM Product Security Incident Response Blog [http://www.ibm.com/blogs/psirt] 

CHANGE HISTORY
 30 April 2015: Original Version Published 

*The CVSS Environment Score is customer environment specific and will ultimately impact the Overall CVSS Score. Customers can evaluate the impact of this vulnerability in their environments by accessing the links in the Reference section of this Security Bulletin. 

DISCLAIMER
According to the Forum of Incident Response and Security Teams (FIRST), the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) is an "industry open standard designed to convey vulnerability severity and help to determine urgency and priority of response." IBM PROVIDES THE CVSS SCORES "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. raw_results.ground_truth: This error may indicate a problem with the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) when launching IBM Rational Software Architect. To resolve this issue, you can try updating the video driver to the newest version, as outdated or incompatible drivers can cause JVM termination errors. Another possible cause could be related to a bug in the Intel Graphic Card driver for Windows XP Service Pack 3. If updating the video driver does not solve the problem, you should contact Rational support for further assistance.

raw_results.answer: Errors when launching an application can arise from various issues, including expired or incorrect licenses, incorrect system date and time settings, corrupted files, compatibility issues with other software, or insufficient system resources.

Title: TECHQA | Q14 passage 2

 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 I am trying to start SPSS for Windows and I receive the following errors:

The current license does not permit this job to continue.

followed by...

Error # 7003
The license for SPSS for Windows has expired.
This command not executed.

Specific symptom number: 213

What is the problem? 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
IF the product presenting the error 7003/symptom 213 is licensed via the Sentinel RMS License Manager, THEN refer to KnowledgeBase resolution 91720.
IF the product presenting error 7003/symptom 213 is licensed with a Single-User or Site license authorization code, THEN go to (A).

(A)
IF your was working normally for more than 14 days prior to presenting error 7003/symptom 213, THEN go to (B)
IF your product has been working for no more than 14 days prior to presenting error 7003/symptom 213, THEN go to (E)

(B) 
Confirm the date and time on the computer are correct.

IF date/time is NOT correct, THEN got to (C)
IF date/time is correct, THEN go to (D). 

(C)
1) Reset the date/time on the computer.
2) Start the IBM SPSS product.

IF the error message persists, THEN go to (D)
IF the error not longer presents, then the problem has been resolved.

(D)
1) Delete (or rename) the file named 'lservrc' found in program installation directory of the IBM SPSS product. 
2) License the IBM SPSS product using the License Authorization Wizard and your product authorization code.

(E)
1) Please contact IBM SPSS Customer Service via email at spsscs@us.ibm.com or via phone at 800-521-1337.
2) Provide IBM SPSS Customer Service with the license authorization code used to license your IBM SPSS product.
3) Request confirmation that the license authorization code is for the correct product.
4) Request confirmation that the license authorization code includes the 'base' module (if applicable).
5) Request confirmation that the license authorization code is not expired

RELATED INFORMATION

Title: TECHQA | Q8 passage 0

 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 I have encountered program crashes and unexplained assertion errors while trying to run a a linear mixed-model analysis (MIXED command) in Statistics 18.0. I know my data is structured properly, with a single row for each data point and with no duplication of the combinations of repeated (within-subject) factor levels within the cases for each subject. However, I get a processor crash and "program terminating" message whenever I try to run the analysisit. Immediately following the log of the MIXED command in the output viewer is the message:

" _ASSERT(qfirst) failed in mxceff "

This message is followed by some messages regarding resources used and the following termination message:

"Warnings
Undefined error #91 - Cannot open text file "C:\Program Files\SPSSInc\PASWStatistics18\lang\en\spss.err": No such file or directory
This is an error from which Statistics cannot recover.
The Statistics run will terminate now."


A colleague has run the same analysis with the same data set on his own computer and not encountered the error. What does the message mean and why is the crash triggered on my computer? 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
A program defect was filed with SPSS Development. Technical Support was unable to replicate the crash. SPSS Development was able to trigger ASSERT_ warnings with program crashes by renaming the spss.err file that was mentioned in the later part of your error messages. They have made program changes to avoid such crashes in the future. However, they were unable to replicate the error in the context of running the customer's MIXED syntax on the customer's data. 

The customer has since reported that all of the computers involved in these crashes had Iolo System Mechanic Professional installed. Subsequent runs of the same analysis on computers without System Mechanic ran without error messages or crashes. Users that experience system crashes with the message:

_ASSERT(qfirst) failed in mxceff

should examine the computer to see if Iolo System Mechanic is installed. If so, try the analysis on a computer without System Mechanic.

RELATED INFORMATION
 Need more help? Our Statistics forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/topics/statistics.html?smartspace=predictive-analytics]


 

HISTORICAL NUMBER
 91124

Title: TECHQA | Q8 passage 3

 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 I am using Statistics 17 was sent a licence file (lservrc) by Technical Support/Licensing to correct a license error.

After saving this file to my Statistics installation directory, starting Statistics will result in error 7001 symptom 18.

I tried right clicking on Statistics then "Run as Administrator" but I still receive the same error. 

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The workaround is to turn off "Hide extension for known files types", then rename lservrc to it's correct state.

For Windows XP - Open "My Computer" > "Tools" > "Folder Options"
For Windows Vista - Open "Computer" > "Organise" > "Folder and Search Options".

Once extensions are viewable, open the Statistics 17 installation directory, default being: C:\Program Files\SPSSinc\Statistics17 or \PASWStatistics17.
Right click the file 'lservrc.txt' / 'lservrc.dat' and rename it to just 'lservrc'
You should now be able to restart Statistics

RELATED INFORMATION
 Need more help? Our AMOS forum is Live! [https://developer.ibm.com/answers/search.html?f=&type=question&redirect=search%2Fsearch&sort=relevance&q=%2B[amos]]

   The COBOL versions of copybooks DFHTCTTE and DFHJCADS are

   copybooks will therefore compile with errors.

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 2

exit; code; 8096; crash; crashes; RTC; RSA; java virtual machine; Work item 166607 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 This technote explains why opening workitem using IBM Rational Team Concert integrated with Rational Software Architect crashes with the error "JVM terminated. Exit code=8096" . It also provides instructions to resolve the issue. 

SYMPTOM
Rational Team Concert crashes with the following JVM terminated error message: 

JVM terminated. Exit code=8096 

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21501371&aid=1]

CAUSE
There are many possible factors that could contribute to the crash the JVM exit code=8096 error message. 

One of possible causes might be a bug in the Intel Graphic Card driver for Windows XP Service Pack 3


ENVIRONMENT
Rational Team Concert version 3.0 and 3.0.1 integrated with Rational Software Architect 

 * Intel(R) Q45/Q43 Express Chipset 
 * Windows XP Service Pack 3

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Upgrade your graphic card driver to the latest one. If upgrading the driver does not fix the issue, contact Rational support..

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 1

RBD; RTC; crashs TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Attempts to use IBM Rational Business Developer connected to an IBM Rational Team Concert server, result in error "Java was started but ... ". 

SYMPTOM
You connected a Rational Team Concert server to Rational Business Developer and you perform actions on the server. Then the Eclipse workbench, java.exe, stops with the following error message. 

Java was started but returned exit code=8096 


[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1]

Examples of actions you can do are: creating a work item and creating a user.

CAUSE
One known cause is due to the video driver and when two different versions of drivers are installed on the machine (i.e 6.14.10.5284 and 6.14.10.5303) 


Note: Other causes are also possible but have not been reported.


ENVIRONMENT
This problem has been seen with ThinkPad laptops



DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Look at the java core. The following entries support the hypothesis of the faulty video drivers. 

 

 1. The last call before the problem relates to the Microsoft gdiplus.dll graphics library. 
    
 2. The correct values passes correctly to the Standard Widget Toolkit (SWT) before the error occurs.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Update the video driver to the newest version 

Link: Intel Download Center [http://downloadcenter.intel.com/]


RELATED INFORMATION
#JVM terminated. Exit code=8096 [https://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21303648]
Internet Explorer v9 or higher is required if the produ [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502701]

Rational Developer for System z Fix Pack 1 (8.5.1.1) for 8.5.1, 8.5.0.1, and 8.5 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24034596]


SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Rational Developer for System z Software Analyzer hangs when
   analyzing a program with many copybooks.
   
   There are two scenarios:
   Common is that the default COBOL rules are selected. The use
   of custom rules is not significant.
   1) Start Software Analyzer while the copybooks were
      downloading - hang with the large number (e.g. 2147483647%)
      at the (bottom right) status area.
   2) Wait until all the status message are done (copybook
      downloads and COBOL syntax parser) in the status area
      (bottom right) and then start Software Analyzer - hang
      with the Running Software Analyzer 100% status at the
      bottom right.
   
   Cause:
   This problem is caused by a deadlock that occurs when
   Software Analyzer is running while the copybooks are being
   loaded
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  The locking of copybook resources collided with resource locking
   of concurrent jobs.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  Modified code to avoid resource-locking collisions with
   concurrent jobs.
   
   The problem listed in this APAR has been resolved with
   IBM Rational Developer for System z V8.5.1.1 Fix Pack which
   is available from the Recommended Fixes support download page:
   http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=2294&uid=swg27006335 [http://www-1.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=2294&uid=swg27006335]
